requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the visual content is necessary
the task is to choose a SMILES expression from a depicted structure, which depends on reading molecular geometry/stereochemistry from the image
asset_registry_record shows an existing primary image and registry_passed=true
multi_step: the solver must interpret the depicted transition-state structure and then map it to one of 6 answer choices
the choices differ by stereochemical annotations such as @ and @@, so matching likely requires more than simple object recognition
the instruction to ignore arrows suggests separating relevant structural information from non-answer visual elements
verifiability: answer_type is short_text and the provided answer exactly matches one listed choice
there are 6 explicit options, so correctness can be checked by exact option/string match
registry_passed=true, answer_present=true, and no registry issues are listed
SMILES output is structured text, which is easier to validate than free-form explanation

decision_reasons: assets are complete: question, answer, and primary image are all present with no registry issues
the problem is strongly image-dependent and the required image asset exists, so core semantics are preserved
verifiability is high because the answer is short structured text and can be checked against explicit options
although the chemistry notation is specialized and stereochemistry-sensitive, these are difficulty risks rather than asset-recovery failures
